vocabulary.definition: A place where coffee and light refreshments are served.
Treatment intended to relieve or heal a disorder.
A child who is five years old.
A teacher of the highest rank in a college or university.
An injury appearing as a discolored area on the skin.
A player in baseball who occupies a base.
A vehicle composed of two wheels held in a frame one behind the other.

corrections.context_hint: The model often fails to recognize contractions, leading to missing apostrophes.
The STT model may misinterpret the compound word due to phonetic blending.
The STT model may confuse similar-sounding words, particularly in rapid speech.
The model may not recognize the need for hyphenation in compound adjectives.

corrections.stt_output: parents had been separated for almost three years but she never expected them too
corrections.intended: Her parents had been separated for almost three years, but she never expected them to
corrections.context_hint: The STT model misinterprets 'Her' as 'parents' and confuses 'to' with 'too' due to phonetic similarity.

corrections.stt_output: of law asked you a thousand times not to leave your dirty socks on the floor
corrections.intended: I've asked you a thousand times not to leave your dirty socks on the floor.
corrections.context_hint: The model mishears 'I've' as 'of law' due to the rapid speech and phonetic overlap.

corrections.stt_output: the waitress was not amused when he or when when eggs
corrections.intended: The waitress was not amused when he ordered green eggs and ham.
corrections.context_hint: The model mishears 'ordered green eggs and ham' as 'or when when eggs' due to unclear pronunciation.

corrections.stt_output: the first basement caught the fly ball
corrections.intended: The first baseman caught the fly ball.
corrections.context_hint: The model confuses 'baseman' with 'basement' due to phonetic similarity.

corrections.stt_output: the chandelier swayed in the breeze sending dust drifting over the tables
corrections.intended: The chandelier swayed in the breeze, sending dust drifting over the tables.
corrections.context_hint: The model fails to include a comma, which alters the flow of the sentence.
The STT model likely mishears the phrase due to phonetic similarity and context.
Phonetic similarity between 'rises' and 'rice' may lead to confusion in transcription.
The STT model may misinterpret the phrase due to phonetic similarity.

corrections.stt_output: sand on the beach as a hop
corrections.intended: sand on the beach as a hobby
corrections.context_hint: Phonetic confusion between 'hop' and 'hobby' may occur in rapid speech.
Phonetic similarity may lead to misinterpretation of the word 'professor'.
The STT model may mishear the vowel sounds in 'bruise' leading to incorrect transcription.

corrections.stt_output: she meant to go to gabarone but mixed up both swans and bowie and ended up in horror
corrections.intended: She meant to go to Gaborone, but mixed up Botswana and Zimbabwe and ended up in Harare
corrections.context_hint: The model confuses 'Gaborone' with 'gabarone' and mishears 'Botswana' and 'Zimbabwe' as 'both swans and bowie' due to phonetic similarity.

corrections.stt_output: he understands why the bird wanted to ride the bice
corrections.intended: He didn't understand why the bird wanted to ride the bicycle.
corrections.context_hint: The model mishears 'bicycle' as 'bice' due to phonetic similarity and possibly unclear enunciation.

corrections.stt_output: she started crying after she finished the cell
corrections.intended: She started crying after she finished the song.
corrections.context_hint: The model mishears 'song' as 'cell' due to phonetic overlap.
The model mishears 'phone' as 'for' due to phonetic similarity.

style.notes: The speaker tends to use informal language and conversational phrases.
